steps.title: Pin down the claim to illustrate
steps.content:
The authors assert that all people naturally have an emotional attachment to where they live. The correct quotation must express a universal emotional bond to home — covering everyone regardless of background.
  • Choice A — "This little book is published for the express purpose of giving a helping hand to suffering and persecuted merit.": Describes the book's purpose; says nothing about emotional attachment to home. ✗
  • Choice B — "To give some idea of the high esteem in which the subject of the following narrative is held, and the strong interest her misfortunes have excited, a few, from the great number of recommendations in her possession, are selected.": Introduces supporting testimonials for Eldridge; no mention of home or attachment. ✗
  • Choice C — "Blessed are the slumbers of the innocent! They are kindlier than balm, and they refresh and gladden the spirit of childhood, like ministerings from a better world.": Describes innocent sleep; does not address home or universal attachment. ✗
  • Choice D — "Home is home, to the lowly as well as the great; and no rank, or color, destroys its sacred character, its power over the mind, and the affections.": Directly asserts that home holds sacred power over everyone — the lowly and the great alike — matching the universal emotional attachment the claim describes. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: The key phrase "to the lowly as well as the great" and "no rank, or color" cover all people universally, which is exactly what the claim requires.
